description: Build system, test infrastructure, and CI/CD pipeline for the MeOS modernization. Sets up CMake, vcpkg, Google Test, Vitest, and GitHub Actions on an empty project skeleton with no dependency on legacy code/.

userStories.title: CMake Build System with vcpkg Integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a CMake build system skeleton with vcpkg dependency management so that the project compiles on any platform.
acceptanceCriteria: Top-level CMakeLists.txt exists with cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.25) and project(meos) declaration
CMakePresets.json exists with configure and build presets for both Debug and Release configurations
vcpkg.json manifest exists at project root declaring external dependencies (at minimum an empty dependencies array)
vcpkg toolchain file is integrated via CMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE in CMakePresets.json
Minimal src/main.cpp exists (int main() { return 0; }) and is built as an executable target named 'meos'
Empty module library targets exist with CMakeLists.txt in src/domain/ and src/util/ (each with at least a placeholder .cpp file so they compile)
Top-level CMakeLists.txt uses add_subdirectory() to include src/domain/ and src/util/
Precompiled headers configured via target_precompile_headers() with common STL headers (string, vector, map, memory)
.gitignore includes build/, out/, vcpkg_installed/, and CMake generated files
cmake --preset default configures without errors
cmake --build --preset default compiles without errors
userStories.notes: VCPKG_ROOT must be set to the vcpkg installation directory

userStories.title: C++ Test Infrastructure with Google Test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Google Test integrated into the CMake build so that migration stories can immediately add C++ unit tests.
acceptanceCriteria: gtest is declared as a dependency in vcpkg.json
CMake option BUILD_TESTING is available and defaults to ON
enable_testing() is called in the top-level CMakeLists.txt
Test executable targets exist per module: test_domain (in tests/domain/) and test_util (in tests/util/)
Each test target links against GTest::gtest_main and its corresponding module library
At least one smoke test per module exists (e.g. TEST(Smoke, Compiles) { EXPECT_TRUE(true); })
ctest discovers and runs all test executables successfully
cmake --build --preset default compiles without errors
ctest --test-dir build --output-on-failure reports all tests passing

userStories.title: Frontend Test Infrastructure with Vitest
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Vitest configured for the React frontend so that frontend tests can be added by migration stories.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ui/web/package.json exists with react, typescript, vitest, and @testing-library/react as dependencies
src/ui/web/tsconfig.json exists with strict TypeScript configuration and JSX support
src/ui/web/vitest.config.ts exists with React/JSX transform configured
At least one smoke test file exists (e.g. src/ui/web/src/__tests__/smoke.test.tsx) that passes
npm run test script in package.json runs vitest and exits successfully
Coverage reporting is configured via vitest config (provider: v8 or istanbul)
npm run build script exists (using vite or tsc) and succeeds
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit exits with code 0)

userStories.title: Linting Configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want linting configured for both C++ and TypeScript so that code quality is enforced consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: .clang-tidy config file exists at project root with at least readability and modernize checks enabled
.clang-format config file exists at project root with a consistent style (e.g. BasedOnStyle: Google or LLVM)
src/ui/web/eslint.config.js (or .eslintrc.*) exists with TypeScript and React rules configured
src/ui/web/.prettierrc exists with consistent formatting rules
npm run lint script exists in src/ui/web/package.json and passes on existing code
npm run format:check script exists in src/ui/web/package.json for CI use
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit exits with code 0)

userStories.title: CI/CD Pipeline - C++ Build and Test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automated C++ builds and tests via GitHub Actions on every push and PR so that regressions are caught early.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/cpp.yml exists and is valid YAML
Workflow triggers on push to main/master and on pull_request events
Matrix strategy builds on both ubuntu-latest and windows-latest
Workflow steps include: checkout, vcpkg bootstrap (via lukka/run-vcpkg or manual), cmake configure, build, and test
vcpkg dependencies are cached via actions/cache or lukka/run-vcpkg built-in caching
clang-tidy runs as a step (at least on the Linux matrix entry)
Build artifacts (compiled executables) are uploaded via actions/upload-artifact
Workflow YAML is syntactically valid (no duplicate keys, proper indentation)

userStories.title: CI/CD Pipeline - Frontend Build and Test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automated frontend builds, tests, and linting via GitHub Actions on every push and PR.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/frontend.yml exists and is valid YAML
Workflow triggers on push to main/master and on pull_request events
Workflow runs in src/ui/web/ directory: npm ci, npm run lint, npm test, npm run build
Workflow uses a Node.js setup step (actions/setup-node) with a specified version
npm dependencies are cached (via actions/cache or actions/setup-node cache option)
Workflow fails if lint, test, or build steps fail
Workflow YAML is syntactically valid (no duplicate keys, proper indentation)
